title: Techmap: Failed to map cell
body:
## Techmap issue

Hi all,
I am trying to make YOSYS map some custom blocks. Just out of testing, I am trying to make it recognize a simple multiplier.  The idea was to define a NxN bit multiplier but since I got some problems during the process, I simplified it to make things easier.
I am using this sv source file as the design to be implemented (`custom_mul_test.sv`):

```
module custom_mul_test
	(
		input logic  in1_0, 
		input logic  in2_0,
		output logic out_0
	);
	assign out_0 = in1_0*in2_0;
endmodule
```
and I am tryting to map the multiplier into this custom module (`mul16x16.v`):
```
module mul16x16 (input in1, in2, output out);
	assign out = in1 * in2;
endmodule
```
I have the following yosys script which reads my sv design and the library file containing my custom module (`[path]` is just a cut for the whole real path, to save space here):
```
read_verilog -sv [path]/custom_mul_test.sv
read_verilog -lib [path]/mul16x16.v

proc
flatten
opt -full
check

stat
```
From the `stat` command, I can get the following result:
```
=== custom_mul_test ===

   Number of wires:                  3
   Number of wire bits:              3
   Number of public wires:           3
   Number of public wire bits:       3
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  1
     $mul                            1

```
So yosys recognizes that the design needs a multiplier thanks to the `-sv` option when running `read_verilog` instruction. Now I get to the point where I want to map that `$mul` to my custom mul. To do that I added the following line to the yosys script file:
```
techmap -map [path]/custom_lib.v -assert
```
where `custom_lib.v` contains the following module:
```
(* techmap_celltype = "$mul" *)
module \$_MUL_ (input in1, in2, output out);
	mul16x16 _TECHMAP_REPLACE_ (.in1(in1),	.in2(in2), .out(out));
endmodule
```
But when `techmap` runs, I got the following output:
```
9. Executing TECHMAP pass (map to technology primitives).

9.1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend:  [path]/custom_lib.v
Parsing Verilog input from `[path]/custom_lib.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\$_MUL_'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

9.2. Continuing TECHMAP pass.
ERROR: (ASSERT MODE) Failed to map cell custom_mul_test.$mul$results/custom_mul_test_sv2v.v:9$1 ($mul).
```
So it seems like the flow could not map the `$mul` cell with my custom one and I am not getting what I am doing wrong. Can somebody point me to the right direction for custom technology mapping? Thank you so much.
